intent: VeloxCRM is a tiered-access customer relationship management platform designed to streamline lead pipeline management and internal productivity. Its core value proposition lies in granular role-based access control (RBAC) paired with a high-fidelity analytics engine. The workflow involves users authenticating via a secure portal to manage individual contact databases, while administrators leverage real-time data visualization dashboards to track team performance and conversion metrics. The platform includes a subscription-gated feature set, utilizing an integrated payment gateway to manage premium service tiers and recurring billing cycles.

entities.name: IdentityPrincipal
entities.description: Represents the authenticated user entity storing encrypted credentials and high-level role claims mapped to RBAC policies.

entities.name: LeadPipelineRecord
entities.description: A state-machine entity that tracks the lifecycle stage, lead scoring metadata, and ownership assignment within the CRM funnel.

entities.name: SubscriptionTier
entities.description: Defines the feature-gate configuration and limits associated with specific service levels, acting as the logic bridge for conditional UI rendering.

entities.name: PaymentBillingContract
entities.description: An entity capturing the integration state with the third-party payment provider, including subscription identifiers and recurring billing lifecycle markers.

entities.name: PerformanceTelemetryPoint
entities.description: A time-series data point capturing granular user activity metrics, stored in the analytics schema for read-only aggregation by the dashboard engine.

entities.name: AccessControlPolicy
entities.description: A granular schema defining permitted operations and resource visibility scopes tied to specific functional roles.

entities.name: ContactInteractionLog
entities.description: An immutable append-only record of all touchpoints between the user and a specific contact, used for audit trails and conversion analysis.

architecture.description: The system utilizes a micro-service-ready architecture centered on a secure PostgreSQL hub. Authentication occurs via JWT issued upon successful credential validation against the IdentityPrincipal entity, which is then passed as a bearer token for stateless API authorization. When a user interacts with the CRM, the request is validated against the AccessControlPolicy to ensure permission granularity. Changes to LeadPipelineRecords trigger synchronous updates to the primary relational database, while asynchronous hooks push event-driven telemetry to the PerformanceTelemetryPoint schema, which resides on a read-only replica to decouple analytic heavy-lifting from transactional operations. Subscription state is synchronized via Stripe Webhooks; upon receiving a lifecycle event, the backend validates the payload against the PaymentBillingContract and updates the SubscriptionTier, which immediately propagates to the frontend for conditional UI rendering of gated CRM features.

validation_rules: user must have read permission on lead records

endpoints.summary: Create a new lead pipeline record

validation_rules: stage must be a valid pipeline state

endpoints.summary: List available subscription tiers and feature gates

validation_rules: user must be authorized for admin analytics
payload must contain valid stripe signature

endpoints.summary: Retrieve access control policies for role-based authorization

rules.description: Ensure LeadPipelineRecord state transitions follow the defined business funnel sequence.
rules.logic: Validate the requested state transition against the current state; reject if the transition violates linear advancement (e.g., Closed-Lost to Qualified).

rules.description: Synchronize SubscriptionTier based on PaymentBillingContract status.
rules.logic: Upon receipt of a payment lifecycle event, match the subscription identifier in PaymentBillingContract and update the associated SubscriptionTier to reflect the current service level eligibility.

rules.description: Enforce granular access control for CRM resource operations.
rules.logic: Intercept all API requests, extract JWT claims, and verify if the identity possesses sufficient scope in the AccessControlPolicy for the requested resource operation.
Deny all DELETE and UPDATE operations on ContactInteractionLog records once persisted; allow only READ-only access.

rules.description: Decouple performance telemetry from transactional database integrity.
rules.logic: Validate incoming telemetry schema and emit an event to the asynchronous processing queue; prevent write failures from impacting the main transaction.

rules.description: Validate authorization scope during user authentication.
rules.logic: Map IdentityPrincipal claims against AccessControlPolicy scopes before issuing the JWT to ensure token constraints are correctly initialized.
